title: Noise & Vibration Cost Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tool to quantify the total cost of noise and vibration in manufacturing environments, based on ISO 2631, ISO 9612, Lean, and Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Number of workers exposed
inputs.businessContext: Total headcount in the noise/vibration zone (e.g., assembly line, press shop).

inputs.label: Average daily exposure hours
inputs.businessContext: Per worker, typical shift duration in the affected area.

inputs.label: Noise level (A-weighted)
inputs.businessContext: Measured at operator position per ISO 9612. 85 dB(A) is typical action level.

inputs.label: Vibration level (RMS acceleration)
inputs.businessContext: Weighted acceleration per ISO 2631-1. Hand-arm or whole-body.

inputs.label: Machine runtime per day
inputs.businessContext: Total operating hours of the primary noise/vibration source.

inputs.label: Annual maintenance cost per machine
inputs.businessContext: Current average spend on repairs and parts for each affected machine.

inputs.label: Number of machines in zone
inputs.businessContext: Count of equipment generating noise/vibration in the assessed area.

inputs.label: Average worker annual salary (fully loaded)
inputs.businessContext: Includes wages, benefits, overhead. Used to estimate productivity loss.

inputs.label: Hearing protection cost per worker per year
Current defect rate due to vibration
inputs.businessContext: Estimated percentage of parts affected by vibration-induced quality issues (e.g., surface finish, tolerances).

inputs.label: Annual production volume
inputs.businessContext: Total units produced per year in the assessed line.

inputs.label: Cost per defect (rework + scrap)
inputs.businessContext: Average cost to rework or scrap a defective unit.

inputs.label: Machine power consumption
inputs.businessContext: Average power draw per machine during operation.

inputs.label: Vibration reduction investment (one-time)
inputs.businessContext: Budget for dampers, isolators, foundations, or redesign.

inputs.label: Noise reduction investment (one-time)
inputs.businessContext: Budget for enclosures, silencers, acoustic panels.

inputs.label: ISO standard compliance level
inputs.businessContext: Select the most relevant standard for threshold evaluation.

rules.message: Worker exposure hours cannot exceed machine runtime hours.
Defect rate must be between 0% and 50%.
Either noise or vibration should be above typical background to justify assessment.

thresholds.alert: Noise level exceeds 85 dB(A) action level per ISO 9612. Hearing conservation program required.
Vibration level in caution zone (ISO 2631). Health surveillance recommended.
Vibration level exceeds danger threshold (ISO 2631). Immediate engineering controls required.
Defect rate above 5% indicates significant vibration impact on quality. Six Sigma DMAIC project recommended.

formulas.label: Productivity loss cost (noise + vibration)
formulas.description: Based on ISO 9612 and literature: 0.5% productivity loss per dB(A) above 80, plus 1% per 0.1 m/s² above 0.3.

formulas.label: Health & PPE cost
formulas.description: Direct cost of hearing protection, audiometric testing, and vibration health surveillance.

formulas.label: Quality defect cost due to vibration
formulas.description: Cost of rework and scrap attributed to vibration-induced defects.

formulas.label: Excess energy cost due to vibration
formulas.description: Vibration increases friction and energy consumption. Estimated 5% increase per 1 m/s² above 0.5.
formulas.expression: number_of_machines * machine_power_kw * machine_runtime_hours_per_day * 365 * energy_cost_per_kwh * (0.05 * MAX(0, vibration_level_ms2 - 0.5))

formulas.label: Excess maintenance cost due to vibration
formulas.description: Vibration accelerates wear. Estimated 10% increase per 1 m/s² above 0.3.

formulas.label: Total annual loss from noise & vibration
formulas.description: Sum of all direct and indirect costs.

formulas.label: Return on investment (5-year)
formulas.description: Net present value of investing in noise and vibration reduction, assuming 50% reduction in losses.

primary.label: Total annual loss from noise & vibration

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Absenteeism due to noise/vibration
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated 2-5% additional absenteeism cost not captured in direct productivity loss.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Regulatory non-compliance fines
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Potential OSHA/EU fines for exceeding noise/vibration limits.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Increased turnover costs
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Higher turnover in high noise/vibration areas (recruitment, training).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Brand reputation damage
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Negative perception from poor working conditions.

suggestedActions.description: Implement engineering controls for vibration (isolators, dampers) on machines exceeding 1.15 m/s².
Install acoustic enclosures or barriers for noise sources above 85 dB(A).
Initiate a Six Sigma DMAIC project to reduce vibration-induced defects by 50%.
Establish a predictive maintenance program using vibration monitoring.
Rotate workers to reduce daily exposure hours below 8 hours.
suggestedActions.estimatedBenefit: Reduced health risk and potential liability

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data confidence adjusted total loss
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total annual loss multiplied by a confidence factor (0.7 to 1.0) based on input completeness and precision.
